headline: Seeds Collected to Save World's Rarest Tree
meta_description: Conservationists have collected seeds from the last known wild Dendroseris neriifolia on Chile's Robinson Crusoe Island to prevent total extinction.
summary_short: The last wild tree of an endangered Chilean species has had its seeds collected and cultivated, offering a lifeline against extinction.
body:
On Robinson Crusoe Island in the South Pacific, a solitary tree clings to a clifftop—the final surviving wild individual of Dendroselis neriifolia. With no others of its kind left in nature, this tree represents both a loss and a critical opportunity for conservation.

Recognizing the urgency, conservationists have recently collected seeds from this lone survivor and begun cultivation trials. The effort represents a powerful act of hope: even when a species has been reduced to a single wild plant, dedicated work can preserve it. By establishing populations grown from these seeds, researchers are working to bring the species back from the brink, ensuring that future generations might once again see this remarkable Chilean tree flourishing in its native habitat.
